title: Zostera minor
intro: Zostera minor, also known as dwarf eelgrass or small eelgrass, is a type of seagrass that belongs to the family Zosteraceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Eastern medicine to promote overall health and well-being, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties that may aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and protect against oxidative stress. Bioactive constituents of Zostera minor include phenolic compounds, alkaloids, and sterols, which have been isolated and studied for their potential medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Zostera minor is often prepared as a decoction or used in combination with other herbs to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, although modern herbal preparations may also include extracts and capsules.
taxonomy: Zostera minor is classified as a species of seagrass, belonging to the family Zosteraceae. It is a type of monocotyledonous flowering plant, characterized by its long, narrow leaves and ability to thrive in shallow coastal waters. Zostera minor is often found in temperate and subtropical marine environments, where it provides essential habitat and food for various aquatic species. The plant's classification also includes its genus Zostera, which comprises several species of seagrasses found in different parts of the world. Zostera minor is considered a relatively rare species, but its specific classification is an essential aspect of understanding its ecological role.
